title: Of Yui 3I and Other Tangents
wikitext:
[]: Of Yui, 3I, and Other Tangents             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Tue Mar 06, 2007 6:13 pm Split from "Gendo's Mysterious Induction" , lol. --Reichu Ugh, this is making my head hurt again... Lemme see if I got this right... Yui was anti-instrumentality from the beginning and brought Gendo and Fuyutsuki along, correct? They develop evas to defend humanity from angels, but not take part in SEELE's plan, but after Yui was lost in Eva-01, Gendo changed plans and wanted to proceed with instrumentality, or at least his own version that would reunite him with ol' Yui. Did I get that right? It's been bugging me for a while.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Mar 06, 2007 6:46 pm Good_Noodle wrote: Did I get that right? It's been bugging me for a while.

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Tue Mar 06, 2007 7:06 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Yui opposes complementation, but her goals involved initiating Third Impact anyway, but having a different outcome for it than SEELE intended.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Tue Mar 06, 2007 9:03 pm Good_Noodle wrote: Ugh, this is making my head hurt again... Lemme see if I got this right... Yui was anti-instrumentality from the beginning and brought Gendo and Fuyutsuki along, correct? They develop evas to defend humanity from angels, but not take part in SEELE's plan, but after Yui was lost in Eva-01, Gendo changed plans and wanted to proceed with instrumentality, or at least his own version that would reunite him with ol' Yui. Did I get that right? It's been bugging me for a while.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Tue Mar 06, 2007 10:56 pm just watch EoE more carefully and its easy to see thats what he wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 6:23 am Fearthebait wrote: just watch EoE more carefully and its easy to see thats what he wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Wed Mar 07, 2007 10:39 am Opteron-O3 wrote: Fearthebait wrote: just watch EoE more carefully and its easy to see thats what he wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 4:22 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: Opteron-O3 wrote: Fearthebait wrote: just watch EoE more carefully and its easy to see thats what he wanted.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 4:43 pm yah, thats why he did everything. Now i am at a crossroads, end the discussion here, or go into detail about how his selfishness caused the events within the series using complicated vocabulary.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 4:46 pm Fearthebait wrote: yah, thats why he did everything. Now i am at a crossroads, end the discussion here, or go into detail about how his selfishness caused the events within the series using complicated vocabulary.

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 4:52 pm Gendo only cared about himself in the regard that he would destroy/liquify the whole world in return for being with Yui again. Its obvious he had severe selfishness issues that up until his death he never overcame.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 5:14 pm Fearthebait wrote: Gendo only cared about himself in the regard that he would destroy/liquify the whole world in return for being with Yui again. Its obvious he had severe selfishness issues that up until his death he never overcame.

[]: If I'm repeating myself, just ignore this! ^^;             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 07, 2007 6:41 pm Part of the reason Gendou was so Yui-focused (I think, at least) was the fact that "I'm not used to being liked.", since Yui thought he was "a sweet person." (Which I think proves that Gendou isn't as "evil" as people make him out to be, the man DOES have a heart, he just doesn't want to always show it!) But with not being liked, hmm...Now who else does that sound like? ("What'll I do if people hate me? What should I do?") Can you say like father, like son? Plus that one pic of Gendou from the OP (and 21'?) looks a bit like Shinji when older.... (Though I personally think Shinji would look MUCH cuter than "daddy-dearest" is. :P)

[]:             Quote Post by Fearthebait » Wed Mar 07, 2007 7:08 pm interesting point, i just wish we knew even a tiny bit more about young Gendo

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Mar 07, 2007 7:39 pm Fearthebait wrote: Gendo only cared about himself in the regard that he would destroy/liquify the whole world in return for being with Yui again.

[]:             Quote Post by Good_Noodle » Wed Mar 07, 2007 8:00 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Fearthebait wrote: Gendo only cared about himself in the regard that he would destroy/liquify the whole world in return for being with Yui again.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 07, 2007 8:42 pm Good_Noodle wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Fearthebait wrote: Gendo only cared about himself in the regard that he would destroy/liquify the whole world in return for being with Yui again.

[]:             Quote Post by Opteron-O3 » Wed Mar 07, 2007 8:45 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Part of the reason Gendou was so Yui-focused (I think, at least) was the fact that "I'm not used to being liked.", since Yui thought he was "a sweet person." (Which I think proves that Gendou isn't as "evil" as people make him out to be, the man DOES have a heart, he just doesn't want to always show it!) But with not being liked, hmm...Now who else does that sound like? ("What'll I do if people hate me? What should I do?") Can you say like father, like son? Plus that one pic of Gendou from the OP (and 21'?) looks a bit like Shinji when older.... (Though I personally think Shinji would look MUCH cuter than "daddy-dearest" is. :P)
